title: InvisiCaps: The Fil-C Capability Model
excerpt: Fil-C ensures memory safety of all operations in the C and C++ language. The hardest part of C memory safety is pointer safety. content_html:
Fil-C ensures memory safety of all operations in the C and C++ language. The hardest part of C memory safety is pointer safety. Fil-C achieves pointer safety using a capability system for pointers. Specifically, each pointer dynamically tracks what object in memory it should be allowed to access, and using that pointer to access any memory that is not that object is dynamically prohibited.

Achieving memory safety using a pointer capability model means:

  • Prohibiting accesses that are out of bounds of the object the pointer is allowed to access.

  • Prohibiting accesses to freed objects.

  • Prohibiting writes to readonly data.

  • Prohibiting reads and writes to non-data objects, such as function pointers, or "special" objects internal to the Fil-C runtime (like the innards of threads or jmp_bufs).

  • Prohibiting reads or writes that would corrupt Fil-C's understanding of any pointer's capability, leading to failure to meaningfully enforce any of these rules.

In addition to memory safety, Fil-C's other goal is fanatical compatibility. This means that Fil-C's capability model also has to allow most (or ideally all) "safe" uses of C pointers; that is, idioms that are in widespread use and don't lead to exploitation unless they violate the above rules. This means even supporting uses of pointers that the C spec deems to be undefined behavior. And it means preserving sizeof(T*) to be the same as what you'd expect - i.e. 8 bytes on 64-bit platforms.

This article describes how Fil-C's capability mode, called InvisiCaps (Invisible Capabilities), achieves all of these goals while also providing reasonable performance and memory usage.

How Hard Can It Be?

Designing a capability model that satisfies these requirements is sufficiently hard that it is an area of active research. Fil-C has now gone through multiple capability systems; InvisiCaps are just the latest. To appreciate the difficulty of this journey, let's review the previous models and why they were abandoned:

  • PLUT (Pointer Lower Upper Type): This required making pointers 256 bits (four 64-bit pointers). This model required knowing the type of each allocation up front and didn't meaningfully support unions. PLUTs were also not thread-safe, which meant that they were not memory safe in multi-threaded programs. PLUTs did not require a GC; they were coupled with an isoheap allocator. Use-after-free was not an error (was not detected), but could not be used to corrupt capabilities.

  • SideCaps (Sidecar plus Capability): This was an ingenious capability model that solved the thread safety of PLUTs. SideCaps were a heroic racy atomic protocol that allowed 256 bits of data to be encoded atomically using only 128-bit atomic operations. I am very proud of SideCaps, but they were awful. They were extremely slow (back then, Fil-C was 200x slower than normal C). And, they required knowing the type at allocation time, so they did not meaningfully support unions. Use-after free was not an error (was not detected), but could not be used to corrupt any SideCaps.

  • MonoCaps (Monotonic Capabilities): The main advantage of switching to MonoCaps was that the type of an object did not have to be determined at allocation time, but could be monotonically revealed as the program used the object. This allowed somewhat meaningful union usage, and obviated the need to infer the type at malloc callsites. MonoCaps also reduced the perf overhead of Fil-C to about 10x, reduced pointer size to 128 bits, unlocked C++ support, and added the ability to deterministically panic on use-after-free rather than only preventing capability corruption on use-after-free. MonoCaps also coincided with the introduction of Fil's Unbelievable Garbage Collector. It's not possible to do MonoCaps without a GC.

InvisiCaps are a major improvement over MonoCaps:

  • InvisiCaps allow for 64-bit pointers on 64-bit systems (and would allow for 32-bit pointers if Fil-C supported 32-bit systems).

  • InvisiCaps have thus far allowed for a reduction of performance overhead to about 4x in the bad cases. There are still performance optimization opportunities with InvisiCaps that I haven't explored so they're likely to get faster still.

  • InvisiCaps allow for meaningful union usage. The type of a memory location can change over the lifetime of that location.

InvisiCaps achieve this without sacrificing thread safety of the capability model.

What Are InvisiCaps Most Like?

InvisiCaps can be thought of as a practical-to-implement and totally thread-safe variant of SoftBound. InvisiCaps are heavily inspired by that work. Unlike SoftBound, InvisiCaps don't allow memory safety escapes by racing on pointers, and they allow for making all features of C and C++ safe, including subtle stuff like function pointer usage. The greatest similarity between SoftBound and InvisiCaps is that they both place the capability metadata "outside" of the address space visible to the program.

InvisiCaps can also be thought of as a software implementation of CHERI. Unlike CHERI, InvisiCaps are more compatible (pointers are 64-bit, not 128-bit or 256-bit) and InvisiCaps have a more deterministic use-after-free story.

The Intuition Of InvisiCaps

Let's start with a simple intuition for how InvisiCaps work, without worrying about how a pointer and its capability are stored in memory. Let's just consider:

  • A pointer in a local variable, not in memory. We'll call this a flight pointer.

  • The layout of the object that the pointer points at.

For our example, let's consider a pointer that points into the middle of an object.

The flight pointer has two parts:

  • The lower bound ptr. This gives us the lower bounds for the purpose of bounds checking pointer accesses. It also pointers right above the object header, which contains:

    • The upper bound ptr. We'll use this for the upper bounds check. This has to be at least as large as the lower bound ptr. It can be exactly equal to the lower bound ptr, for objects that cannot be accessed at all (like free objects or special objects).

    • The aux word. We'll come back to this! It's sneaky!

  • The ptr intval. This is the raw integer value of the pointer as visible to the C program. When the C program reasons about the value of the pointer (using arithmetic, casts, printing the pointer, comparing the pointer to other pointers, etc), it only ever sees the intval.

We refer to the two parts of the flight ptr as the lower and the intval for short (though be warned, the Fil-C source code usually refers to the intval as the "ptr").

The lower cannot be modified by the C program; you get a lower when you allocate an object and then any pointer derived from the pointer that came out of the allocator has that lower. The lower is trusted by the Fil-C runtime, since it's used for bounds checks (both directly, for the lower bounds check, and indirectly, for all checks that rely on the object header just below where the lower points).

The intval can be modified by the C program and isn't trusted by the Fil-C runtime at all.

Every memory access involves an untrusted intval, indicating the location that the program wishes to access, and a trusted lower, indicating what addresses this particular pointer can access (and how).

As a bonus feature, the lower can be NULL. Pointers with NULL lower cannot be accessed (the access always traps with an error saying you have a NULL lower).

If pointers could only ever live in local variables and could not be stored to the heap, then that would be the whole story!

InvisiCaps At Rest

When a pointer is stored to the heap, we call this a pointer at rest (or rest pointer). Like a pointer in flight, a pointer at rest must know its lower and intval. InvisiCaps for pointers at rest achieve the following goals:

  • If you store a pointer to the heap and load it back as an integer, you get the intval.

  • If you store an integer to the heap and load it back as a pointer, you get a pointer with a null lower, or whatever lower the last pointer stored to that location had. You never get an invalid or corrupted lower.

  • It's not possible to access a pointer at the location where the lower is stored; no capability has the locations used for storing lowers (or any other Fil-C metadata) within its bounds. This is the key property of InvisiCaps, and the thing that makes them "invisible" -- the C program just sees the intval portion of pointers.

  • Racing on pointer accesses results in the program loading some valid lower (it just might not be the lower you wanted, so you might trap on access).

  • Atomic pointer accesses (using _Atomic, volatile, or std::atomic) are really atomic and lock-free.

The key insight behind how this is achieved is the InvisiCaps inductive hypothesis:

Every flight pointer's lower points to the top of an object header whose aux word contains a way to get the lowers for all pointers stored to that object's payload.

The aux word of an object that has no pointers is simply NULL. But if an object had any pointers stored into it, an aux allocation is allocated, which has the same size as the object's payload. The aux word then points at the aux allocation. If we ignore atomic pointers for now, all pointers at rest in the object have their lower in the aux allocation and their intval in the object payload.

In this example, let's consider two objects. Object #1 has a pointer at rest in its payload, which points at Object #2. So, its aux word points at an aux alloation that contains that pointer's lower. The pointer's intval is in the object's payload. Note that the flight pointer in our example points at the pointer at rest in object #1.

Hence, all nonatomic pointer loads and stores access both the aux allocation (via the aux word, which is just below where lower points) and the object payload. Stores may cause the aux allocation to be lazily created.

Note that this is approach is very friendly to garbage collection:

  • If the aux word isn't set, then the GC treats the object as a leaf (it has no outgoing pointers from the GC's perspective).

  • If the aux word is set, then the GC only looks for pointers in the aux allocation.

Atomic InvisiCaps

But what if a pointer at rest is _Atomic, volatile, std::atomic, or was stored using any other type or mechanism that clang considers to require atomicity?

The aux allocation can contain either lowers or atomic box pointers. The type of entry in the aux allocation is determined by the low bit of that entry (zero indicates lower, one indicates atomic box pointer). Atomic boxes are 16-byte, 16-byte-aligned allocations that contain a flight pointer that we store using 128-bit atomics. Or, if the system does not support double-CAS, we could use 64-bit atomics on the atomic box pointer itself and allocate a new atomic box every time an atomic mutation happens (luckily both X86_64 and ARM64 have 128-bit atomics, so that's not necessary).

Note that when a pointer at rest is atomic, the payload contains a copy of the intval, purely for allowing integer loads to see the intval. However, racing an integer access against a pointer access may result in time travel, but cannot result in the atomic box being corrupted -- it'll just be a logic error.

This approach allows Fil-C to support atomic InvisiCaps when the program wants atomicity, while using a cheaper approach to pointers when atomicity is not requested.

Additional Considerations

The aux word uses only its lower 48 bits for storing the aux allocation pointer; the upper 16 bits are used for flags and additional data. That additional data is helpful for nonstandard object allocations as well as special objects like functions, threads, and other builtin types provided by the Fil-C runtime. Let's consider some examples.

Aligned Allocations

If an object is allocated using posix_memalign or similar API and a sufficiently large alignment is requested, then the the lower minus the object header size points inside an aligned allocation. So, the GC can't work out what the base of the allocation is just simply by subtracting the object header size from the lower. The extra flags in the aux word have enough space to encode the object alignment, which can be used to work out the true base of the allocation.

Memory Mapping

mmap and Sys-V shared memory require special treatment. The GC knows how to manage even those allocations, but special care must be taken with them. The aux word has flags that are set if an object requires such special care.

Function Pointers

Function pointers have an intval that points at the actual function entrypoint, while the lower points to a function capability that:

  • Has an upper bound equal to the lower, which prevents all data accesses.

  • Has flags set to indicate that the capability really is a function.

  • The remainder of the aux word is used to indicate the true function entrypoint, so that function calls can check if the function pointer really points at the entrypoint.

Function pointer casts are handled dynamically (the Fil-C calling convention dynamically resolves mismatches in types passed by the caller and types expected by the callee).

Threads

Fil-C's internal thread abstraction, called zthread, is used internally by musl's and glibc's pthread implementations. Because the zthread contains a lot of internal runtime state, it needs to be specially protected. A pointer to a zthread - or to any of the other builtin types - has the following structure:

  • The lower points at the internal zthread payload.

  • The upper bounds is equal to lower, so no data accesses are possible.

  • The flags in the aux word indicate that this is really a zthread object.

All builtin functions that take a zthread pointer check that the pointer they are given is really a zthread pointer by looking at the aux word's flags.

Freed Objects

Freeing an object results in deterministic panics when accessing the freed memory. This is simple to achieve:

  • Freeing sets the pointer's upper bound to be equal to lower, thus preventing all accesses.

  • A free bit is set in the aux word so that the diagnostic message printed when the program panics indicates that it was because of use after free.

Conclusion

Fil-C uses a capability model for pointers to ensure memory safety. To ensure maximum compatibility, Fil-C's InvisiCap capability model gives the illusion that pointers are just 64-bit on 64-bit systems and allows flexible reinterpretation of memory type (including violations of the active member union rule) while preserving the soundness of the capability model itself. No matter how evil the program is, it will either get a memory safe outcome (all pointer accesses are in bounds of a capability that the pointer legitimately had) or a Fil-C panic.

Additional reading:

  • InvisiCaps by Example

  • Explanation of Fil-C Disassembly

  • Fil's Unbelievable C Compiler

  • Garbage In, Memory Safety Out!

blocks.html_en: • Prohibiting reads or writes that would corrupt Fil-C's understanding of any pointer's capability, leading to failure to meaningfully enforce any of these rules.

blocks.html_en: In addition to memory safety, Fil-C's other goal is fanatical compatibility. This means that Fil-C's capability model also has to allow most (or ideally all) "safe" uses of C pointers; that is, idioms that are in widespread use and don't lead to exploitation unless they violate the above rules. This means even supporting uses of pointers that the C spec deems to be undefined behavior. And it means preserving sizeof(T*) to be the same as what you'd expect - i.e. 8 bytes on 64-bit platforms.

sentences.en: In addition to memory safety, Fil-C's other goal is fanatical compatibility.
sentences.zh: 除了内存安全之外，Fil-C的另一个目标是狂热的兼容性。

sentences.en: This means that Fil-C's capability model also has to allow most (or ideally all) "safe" uses of C pointers; that is, idioms that are in widespread use and don't lead to exploitation unless they violate the above rules.

sentences.en: This means even supporting uses of pointers that the C spec deems to be undefined behavior.
sentences.zh: 这意味着甚至支持使用 C 规范认为是未定义行为的指针。

sentences.en: And it means preserving sizeof(T*) to be the same as what you'd expect - i.e. 8 bytes on 64-bit platforms.
sentences.zh: 这意味着将 sizeof(T*) 保留为与您期望的相同 - 即 64 位平台上的 8 个字节。

blocks.html_en: This article describes how Fil-C's capability mode, called InvisiCaps (Invisible Capabilities), achieves all of these goals while also providing reasonable performance and memory usage.

blocks.html_en: How Hard Can It Be?

sentences.en: How Hard Can It Be?
sentences.zh: 这有多难？

blocks.html_en: Designing a capability model that satisfies these requirements is sufficiently hard that it is an area of active research. Fil-C has now gone through multiple capability systems; InvisiCaps are just the latest. To appreciate the difficulty of this journey, let's review the previous models and why they were abandoned:

sentences.en: Designing a capability model that satisfies these requirements is sufficiently hard that it is an area of active research.
sentences.zh: 设计满足这些要求的能力模型非常困难，因此它是一个活跃的研究领域。

sentences.en: Fil-C has now gone through multiple capability systems; InvisiCaps are just the latest.
sentences.zh: Fil-C现已经历了多个能力体系； InvisiCaps 是最新的。

sentences.en: To appreciate the difficulty of this journey, let's review the previous models and why they were abandoned:
sentences.zh: 为了体会这一旅程的困难，让我们回顾一下以前的模型以及它们被放弃的原因：

blocks.html_en: • PLUT (Pointer Lower Upper Type): This required making pointers 256 bits (four 64-bit pointers). This model required knowing the type of each allocation up front and didn't meaningfully support unions. PLUTs were also not thread-safe, which meant that they were not memory safe in multi-threaded programs. PLUTs did not require a GC; they were coupled with an isoheap allocator. Use-after-free was not an error (was not detected), but could not be used to corrupt capabilities.

sentences.en: PLUT (Pointer Lower Upper Type): This required making pointers 256 bits (four 64-bit pointers).
sentences.zh: PLUT（指针下上位类型）：这需要将指针制作为 256 位（四个 64 位指针）。

sentences.en: This model required knowing the type of each allocation up front and didn't meaningfully support unions.
sentences.zh: 该模型需要预先了解每个分配的类型，并且对联合没有任何意义的支持。

sentences.en: PLUTs were also not thread-safe, which meant that they were not memory safe in multi-threaded programs.
sentences.zh: PLUT 也不是线程安全的，这意味着它们在多线程程序中不是内存安全的。

sentences.en: PLUTs did not require a GC; they were coupled with an isoheap allocator.
sentences.zh: PLUT 不需要 GC；它们与 isoheap 分配器结合在一起。

sentences.zh: 释放后使用不是错误（未检测到），但不能用于破坏功能。

blocks.html_en: • SideCaps (Sidecar plus Capability): This was an ingenious capability model that solved the thread safety of PLUTs. SideCaps were a heroic racy atomic protocol that allowed 256 bits of data to be encoded atomically using only 128-bit atomic operations. I am very proud of SideCaps, but they were awful. They were extremely slow (back then, Fil-C was 200x slower than normal C). And, they required knowing the type at allocation time, so they did not meaningfully support unions. Use-after free was not an error (was not detected), but could not be used to corrupt any SideCaps.

sentences.zh: SideCaps（Sidecar plus Capability）：这是一个巧妙的能力模型，解决了PLUT的线程安全问题。

sentences.zh: SideCaps 是一个英雄般的原子协议，它允许仅使用 128 位原子操作对 256 位数据进行原子编码。

sentences.zh: 它们非常慢（当时，Fil-C 比普通 C 慢 200 倍）。

sentences.zh: 而且，他们需要在分配时知道类型，因此他们没有有意义地支持联合。

blocks.html_en: • MonoCaps (Monotonic Capabilities): The main advantage of switching to MonoCaps was that the type of an object did not have to be determined at allocation time, but could be monotonically revealed as the program used the object. This allowed somewhat meaningful union usage, and obviated the need to infer the type at malloc callsites. MonoCaps also reduced the perf overhead of Fil-C to about 10x, reduced pointer size to 128 bits, unlocked C++ support, and added the ability to deterministically panic on use-after-free rather than only preventing capability corruption on use-after-free. MonoCaps also coincided with the introduction of Fil's Unbelievable Garbage Collector. It's not possible to do MonoCaps without a GC.

sentences.zh: 这允许在某种程度上有意义的联合使用，并且避免了在 malloc 调用站点推断类型的需要。

sentences.zh: MonoCaps 也与 Fil 的难以置信的垃圾收集器的推出同时发生。

blocks.html_en: What Are InvisiCaps Most Like?

sentences.en: What Are InvisiCaps Most Like?
sentences.zh: InvisiCaps 最喜欢什么？

blocks.html_en: InvisiCaps can be thought of as a practical-to-implement and totally thread-safe variant of SoftBound. InvisiCaps are heavily inspired by that work. Unlike SoftBound, InvisiCaps don't allow memory safety escapes by racing on pointers, and they allow for making all features of C and C++ safe, including subtle stuff like function pointer usage. The greatest similarity between SoftBound and InvisiCaps is that they both place the capability metadata "outside" of the address space visible to the program.

sentences.en: InvisiCaps can be thought of as a practical-to-implement and totally thread-safe variant of SoftBound.
sentences.zh: InvisiCaps 可以被认为是 SoftBound 的一个实用且完全线程安全的变体。

sentences.en: InvisiCaps are heavily inspired by that work.
sentences.zh: InvisiCaps 深受这项工作的启发。

sentences.en: Unlike SoftBound, InvisiCaps don't allow memory safety escapes by racing on pointers, and they allow for making all features of C and C++ safe, including subtle stuff like function pointer usage.
sentences.zh: 与 SoftBound 不同，InvisiCaps 不允许通过指针竞争来逃逸内存安全，并且它们允许使 C 和 C++ 的所有功能都安全，包括诸如函数指针使用之类的微妙内容。

sentences.en: The greatest similarity between SoftBound and InvisiCaps is that they both place the capability metadata "outside" of the address space visible to the program.
sentences.zh: SoftBound 和 InvisiCaps 之间最大的相似之处在于，它们都将功能元数据放置在程序可见的地址空间“外部”。

blocks.html_en: InvisiCaps can also be thought of as a software implementation of CHERI. Unlike CHERI, InvisiCaps are more compatible (pointers are 64-bit, not 128-bit or 256-bit) and InvisiCaps have a more deterministic use-after-free story.

sentences.en: InvisiCaps can also be thought of as a software implementation of CHERI.
sentences.zh: InvisiCaps 也可以被视为 CHERI 的软件实现。

sentences.en: Unlike CHERI, InvisiCaps are more compatible (pointers are 64-bit, not 128-bit or 256-bit) and InvisiCaps have a more deterministic use-after-free story.
sentences.zh: 与 CHERI 不同，InvisiCaps 更具兼容性（指针是 64 位，而不是 128 位或 256 位），并且 InvisiCaps 具有更具确定性的释放后使用故事。

blocks.html_en:
• The lower bound ptr. This gives us the lower bounds for the purpose of bounds checking pointer accesses. It also pointers right above the object header, which contains:

    • The upper bound ptr. We'll use this for the upper bounds check. This has to be at least as large as the lower bound ptr. It can be exactly equal to the lower bound ptr, for objects that cannot be accessed at all (like free objects or special objects).

    • The aux word. We'll come back to this! It's sneaky!

blocks.html_en: We refer to the two parts of the flight ptr as the lower and the intval for short (though be warned, the Fil-C source code usually refers to the intval as the "ptr").

blocks.html_en: The lower cannot be modified by the C program; you get a lower when you allocate an object and then any pointer derived from the pointer that came out of the allocator has that lower. The lower is trusted by the Fil-C runtime, since it's used for bounds checks (both directly, for the lower bounds check, and indirectly, for all checks that rely on the object header just below where the lower points).

blocks.html_en: Every memory access involves an untrusted intval, indicating the location that the program wishes to access, and a trusted lower, indicating what addresses this particular pointer can access (and how).

blocks.html_en: As a bonus feature, the lower can be NULL. Pointers with NULL lower cannot be accessed (the access always traps with an error saying you have a NULL lower).

blocks.html_en: InvisiCaps At Rest

sentences.en: InvisiCaps At Rest
sentences.zh: InvisiCaps 静止状态

blocks.html_en: When a pointer is stored to the heap, we call this a pointer at rest (or rest pointer). Like a pointer in flight, a pointer at rest must know its lower and intval. InvisiCaps for pointers at rest achieve the following goals:

sentences.en: When a pointer is stored to the heap, we call this a pointer at rest (or rest pointer).
sentences.zh: 当指针存储到堆时，我们将其称为静止指针（或静止指针）。

sentences.en: Like a pointer in flight, a pointer at rest must know its lower and intval.
sentences.zh: 就像飞行中的指针一样，静止的指针必须知道它的下限和间隔。

sentences.en: InvisiCaps for pointers at rest achieve the following goals:
sentences.zh: 用于静止指针的 InvisiCaps 可实现以下目标：

blocks.html_en: • If you store a pointer to the heap and load it back as an integer, you get the intval.

sentences.en: If you store a pointer to the heap and load it back as an integer, you get the intval.
sentences.zh: 如果您存储指向堆的指针并将其作为整数加载回来，您将获得 intval。

blocks.html_en: If you store a pointer to the heap and load it back as an integer, you get the intval.

sentences.en: If you store a pointer to the heap and load it back as an integer, you get the intval.
sentences.zh: 如果您存储指向堆的指针并将其作为整数加载回来，您将获得 intval。

blocks.html_en: • If you store an integer to the heap and load it back as a pointer, you get a pointer with a null lower, or whatever lower the last pointer stored to that location had. You never get an invalid or corrupted lower.

sentences.en: If you store an integer to the heap and load it back as a pointer, you get a pointer with a null lower, or whatever lower the last pointer stored to that location had.
sentences.zh: 如果将一个整数存储到堆中并将其作为指针加载回来，您将得到一个下限为空的指针，或者存储到该位置的最后一个指针的下限。

sentences.en: You never get an invalid or corrupted lower.
sentences.zh: 您永远不会得到无效或损坏的下层。

blocks.html_en: If you store an integer to the heap and load it back as a pointer, you get a pointer with a null lower, or whatever lower the last pointer stored to that location had. You never get an invalid or corrupted lower.

sentences.en: You never get an invalid or corrupted lower.
sentences.zh: 您永远不会得到无效或损坏的下层。

blocks.html_en: • It's not possible to access a pointer at the location where the lower is stored; no capability has the locations used for storing lowers (or any other Fil-C metadata) within its bounds. This is the key property of InvisiCaps, and the thing that makes them "invisible" -- the C program just sees the intval portion of pointers.

blocks.html_en: Every flight pointer's lower points to the top of an object header whose aux word contains a way to get the lowers for all pointers stored to that object's payload.

sentences.en: Every flight pointer's lower points to the top of an object header whose aux word contains a way to get the lowers for all pointers stored to that object's payload.
sentences.zh: 每个飞行指针的下限都指向对象标头的顶部，该对象标头的 aux 字包含一种获取存储到该对象有效负载的所有指针的下限的方法。

blocks.html_en: The aux word of an object that has no pointers is simply NULL. But if an object had any pointers stored into it, an aux allocation is allocated, which has the same size as the object's payload. The aux word then points at the aux allocation. If we ignore atomic pointers for now, all pointers at rest in the object have their lower in the aux allocation and their intval in the object payload.

sentences.en: The aux word of an object that has no pointers is simply NULL.
sentences.zh: 没有指针的对象的 aux 字就是 NULL。

sentences.en: But if an object had any pointers stored into it, an aux allocation is allocated, which has the same size as the object's payload.
sentences.zh: 但是，如果一个对象存储了任何指针，则会分配一个辅助分配，其大小与该对象的有效负载相同。

sentences.en: The aux word then points at the aux allocation.
sentences.zh: 然后 aux 字指向 aux 分配。

sentences.en: If we ignore atomic pointers for now, all pointers at rest in the object have their lower in the aux allocation and their intval in the object payload.
sentences.zh: 如果我们暂时忽略原子指针，则对象中所有静止的指针都在辅助分配中具有较低值，在对象有效负载中具有 intval。

blocks.html_en: In this example, let's consider two objects. Object #1 has a pointer at rest in its payload, which points at Object #2. So, its aux word points at an aux alloation that contains that pointer's lower. The pointer's intval is in the object's payload. Note that the flight pointer in our example points at the pointer at rest in object #1.

sentences.en: In this example, let's consider two objects.
sentences.zh: 在此示例中，我们考虑两个对象。

sentences.en: Object #1 has a pointer at rest in its payload, which points at Object #2.
sentences.zh: 对象#1 在其有效负载中有一个静态指针，该指针指向对象#2。

sentences.en: So, its aux word points at an aux alloation that contains that pointer's lower.
sentences.zh: 因此，它的 aux 字指向包含该指针的低位的 aux 分配。

sentences.en: The pointer's intval is in the object's payload.
sentences.zh: 指针的 intval 位于对象的有效负载中。

sentences.en: Note that the flight pointer in our example points at the pointer at rest in object #1.
sentences.zh: 请注意，我们示例中的飞行指针指向对象 #1 中静止的指针。

blocks.html_en: Hence, all nonatomic pointer loads and stores access both the aux allocation (via the aux word, which is just below where lower points) and the object payload. Stores may cause the aux allocation to be lazily created.

sentences.en: Hence, all nonatomic pointer loads and stores access both the aux allocation (via the aux word, which is just below where lower points) and the object payload.
sentences.zh: 因此，所有非原子指针加载和存储都访问 aux 分配（通过 aux 字，位于较低点的下方）和对象有效负载。

sentences.en: Stores may cause the aux allocation to be lazily created.
sentences.zh: 存储可能会导致辅助分配被延迟创建。

blocks.html_en: Note that this is approach is very friendly to garbage collection:

sentences.en: Note that this is approach is very friendly to garbage collection:

blocks.html_en: • If the aux word isn't set, then the GC treats the object as a leaf (it has no outgoing pointers from the GC's perspective).

sentences.en: If the aux word isn't set, then the GC treats the object as a leaf (it has no outgoing pointers from the GC's perspective).
sentences.zh: 如果未设置 aux 字，则 GC 会将该对象视为叶子（从 GC 的角度来看，它没有传出指针）。

blocks.html_en: If the aux word isn't set, then the GC treats the object as a leaf (it has no outgoing pointers from the GC's perspective).

sentences.en: If the aux word isn't set, then the GC treats the object as a leaf (it has no outgoing pointers from the GC's perspective).
sentences.zh: 如果未设置 aux 字，则 GC 会将该对象视为叶子（从 GC 的角度来看，它没有传出指针）。

blocks.html_en: • If the aux word is set, then the GC only looks for pointers in the aux allocation.

sentences.en: If the aux word is set, then the GC only looks for pointers in the aux allocation.
sentences.zh: 如果设置了 aux 字，则 GC 仅在 aux 分配中查找指针。

blocks.html_en: If the aux word is set, then the GC only looks for pointers in the aux allocation.

sentences.en: If the aux word is set, then the GC only looks for pointers in the aux allocation.
sentences.zh: 如果设置了 aux 字，则 GC 仅在 aux 分配中查找指针。

blocks.html_en: Atomic InvisiCaps

sentences.en: Atomic InvisiCaps
sentences.zh: 原子隐形帽

blocks.html_en: But what if a pointer at rest is _Atomic, volatile, std::atomic, or was stored using any other type or mechanism that clang considers to require atomicity?

sentences.en: But what if a pointer at rest is _Atomic, volatile, std::atomic, or was stored using any other type or mechanism that clang considers to require atomicity?
sentences.zh: 但是，如果静态指针是 _Atomic、易失性、std::atomic，或者使用 clang 认为需要原子性的任何其他类型或机制存储，该怎么办？

blocks.html_en: The aux allocation can contain either lowers or atomic box pointers. The type of entry in the aux allocation is determined by the low bit of that entry (zero indicates lower, one indicates atomic box pointer). Atomic boxes are 16-byte, 16-byte-aligned allocations that contain a flight pointer that we store using 128-bit atomics. Or, if the system does not support double-CAS, we could use 64-bit atomics on the atomic box pointer itself and allocate a new atomic box every time an atomic mutation happens (luckily both X86_64 and ARM64 have 128-bit atomics, so that's not necessary).

sentences.en: The aux allocation can contain either lowers or atomic box pointers.
sentences.zh: 辅助分配可以包含下级或原子框指针。

sentences.en: The type of entry in the aux allocation is determined by the low bit of that entry (zero indicates lower, one indicates atomic box pointer).
sentences.zh: 辅助分配中的条目类型由该条目的低位确定（0 表示较低，1 表示原子盒指针）。

sentences.en: Atomic boxes are 16-byte, 16-byte-aligned allocations that contain a flight pointer that we store using 128-bit atomics.
sentences.zh: 原子盒是 16 字节、16 字节对齐的分配，其中包含我们使用 128 位原子存储的飞行指针。

sentences.en: Or, if the system does not support double-CAS, we could use 64-bit atomics on the atomic box pointer itself and allocate a new atomic box every time an atomic mutation happens (luckily both X86_64 and ARM64 have 128-bit atomics, so that's not necessary).
sentences.zh: 或者，如果系统不支持双 CAS，我们可以在原子盒指针本身上使用 64 位原子，并在每次原子突变发生时分配一个新的原子盒（幸运的是 X86_64 和 ARM64 都有 128 位原子，所以这不是必需的）。

blocks.html_en: Note that when a pointer at rest is atomic, the payload contains a copy of the intval, purely for allowing integer loads to see the intval. However, racing an integer access against a pointer access may result in time travel, but cannot result in the atomic box being corrupted -- it'll just be a logic error.

sentences.en: Note that when a pointer at rest is atomic, the payload contains a copy of the intval, purely for allowing integer loads to see the intval.
sentences.zh: 请注意，当静态指针是原子指针时，有效负载包含 intval 的副本，纯粹是为了允许整数加载查看 intval。

sentences.en: However, racing an integer access against a pointer access may result in time travel, but cannot result in the atomic box being corrupted -- it'll just be a logic error.
sentences.zh: 然而，将整数访问与指针访问竞争可能会导致时间旅行，但不会导致原子盒被破坏——这只是一个逻辑错误。

blocks.html_en: This approach allows Fil-C to support atomic InvisiCaps when the program wants atomicity, while using a cheaper approach to pointers when atomicity is not requested.

sentences.en: This approach allows Fil-C to support atomic InvisiCaps when the program wants atomicity, while using a cheaper approach to pointers when atomicity is not requested.
sentences.zh: 当程序需要原子性时，这种方法允许 Fil-C 支持原子性 InvisiCaps，而在不要求原子性时使用更便宜的指针方法。

blocks.html_en: Additional Considerations

sentences.en: Additional Considerations
sentences.zh: 其他注意事项

blocks.html_en: The aux word uses only its lower 48 bits for storing the aux allocation pointer; the upper 16 bits are used for flags and additional data. That additional data is helpful for nonstandard object allocations as well as special objects like functions, threads, and other builtin types provided by the Fil-C runtime. Let's consider some examples.

sentences.en: The aux word uses only its lower 48 bits for storing the aux allocation pointer; the upper 16 bits are used for flags and additional data.
sentences.zh: aux 字仅使用其低 48 位来存储 aux 分配指针；高 16 位用于标志和附加数据。

sentences.en: That additional data is helpful for nonstandard object allocations as well as special objects like functions, threads, and other builtin types provided by the Fil-C runtime.
sentences.zh: 这些附加数据对于非标准对象分配以及特殊对象（如函数、线程​​和 Fil-C 运行时提供的其他内置类型）很有帮助。

sentences.en: Let's consider some examples.
sentences.zh: 让我们考虑一些例子。

blocks.html_en: Aligned Allocations

sentences.en: Aligned Allocations
sentences.zh: 调整分配

blocks.html_en: If an object is allocated using posix_memalign or similar API and a sufficiently large alignment is requested, then the the lower minus the object header size points inside an aligned allocation. So, the GC can't work out what the base of the allocation is just simply by subtracting the object header size from the lower. The extra flags in the aux word have enough space to encode the object alignment, which can be used to work out the true base of the allocation.

sentences.en: If an object is allocated using posix_memalign or similar API and a sufficiently large alignment is requested, then the the lower minus the object header size points inside an aligned allocation.
sentences.zh: 如果使用 posix_memalign 或类似的 API 分配对象并且请求足够大的对齐，则较小的值减去对象头大小指向对齐的分配内。

sentences.en: So, the GC can't work out what the base of the allocation is just simply by subtracting the object header size from the lower.

blocks.html_en: Fil-C uses a capability model for pointers to ensure memory safety. To ensure maximum compatibility, Fil-C's InvisiCap capability model gives the illusion that pointers are just 64-bit on 64-bit systems and allows flexible reinterpretation of memory type (including violations of the active member union rule) while preserving the soundness of the capability model itself. No matter how evil the program is, it will either get a memory safe outcome (all pointer accesses are in bounds of a capability that the pointer legitimately had) or a Fil-C panic.

content_html_zh:
Fil-C 确保 C 和 C++ 语言中所有操作的内存安全。 C 内存安全最难的部分是指针安全。 Fil-C 使用指针能力系统实现指针安全。 具体来说，每个指针动态跟踪内存中应该允许其访问的对象，并且动态禁止使用该指针访问不是该对象的任何内存。

使用指针功能模型实现内存安全意味着：

  • 禁止超出允许指针访问的对象范围的访问。
  • 禁止访问已释放的对象。
  • 禁止写入只读数据。
  • 禁止读取和写入非数据对象，例如函数指针或 Fil-C 运行时内部的“特殊”对象（例如线程或 jmp_bufs 的内部结构）。
  • 禁止会破坏 Fil-C 对任何指针功能的理解的读取或写入，从而导致无法有效地执行任何这些规则。

除了内存安全之外，Fil-C的另一个目标是狂热的兼容性。 这意味着 Fil-C 的能力模型还必须允许大多数（或理想情况下所有）“安全”使用 C 指针；即，广泛使用的习语，除非违反上述规则，否则不会导致剥削。 这意味着甚至支持使用 C 规范认为是未定义行为的指针。 这意味着将 sizeof(T*) 保留为与您期望的相同 - 即 64 位平台上的 8 个字节。

本文介绍了 Fil-C 的功能模式（称为 InvisiCaps（隐形功能））如何实现所有这些目标，同时提供合理的性能和内存使用。

这有多难？

设计满足这些要求的能力模型非常困难，因此它是一个活跃的研究领域。 Fil-C现已经历了多个能力体系； InvisiCaps 是最新的。 为了体会这一旅程的困难，让我们回顾一下以前的模型以及它们被放弃的原因：

  • PLUT（指针下上位类型）：这需要将指针制作为 256 位（四个 64 位指针）。 该模型需要预先了解每个分配的类型，并且对联合没有任何意义的支持。 PLUT 也不是线程安全的，这意味着它们在多线程程序中不是内存安全的。 PLUT 不需要 GC；它们与 isoheap 分配器结合在一起。 释放后使用不是错误（未检测到），但不能用于破坏功能。
  • SideCaps（Sidecar plus Capability）：这是一个巧妙的能力模型，解决了PLUT的线程安全问题。 SideCaps 是一个英雄般的原子协议，它允许仅使用 128 位原子操作对 256 位数据进行原子编码。 我对 SideCaps 感到非常自豪，但它们太糟糕了。 它们非常慢（当时，Fil-C 比普通 C 慢 200 倍）。 而且，他们需要在分配时知道类型，因此他们没有有意义地支持联合。 释放后使用不是错误（未检测到），但不能用于损坏任何 SideCap。
  • MonoCaps（单调功能）：切换到 MonoCaps 的主要优点是不必在分配时确定对象的类型，而是可以在程序使用该对象时单调地显示。 这允许在某种程度上有意义的联合使用，并且避免了在 malloc 调用站点推断类型的需要。 MonoCaps 还将 Fil-C 的性能开销减少到大约 10 倍，将指针大小减少到 128 位，解锁了 C++ 支持，并增加了在释放后使用时确定性恐慌的能力，而不仅仅是防止释放后使用时的能力损坏。 MonoCaps 也与 Fil 的难以置信的垃圾收集器的推出同时发生。 没有 GC 就不可能实现 MonoCap。

InvisiCaps 是对 MonoCaps 的重大改进：

  • InvisiCaps 允许在 64 位系统上使用 64 位指针（如果 Fil-C 支持 32 位系统，则将允许使用 32 位指针）。
  • 迄今为止，InvisiCaps 已在不良情况下将性能开销降低至约 4 倍。 InvisiCaps 仍然存在我尚未探索的性能优化机会，因此它们可能会变得更快。
  • InvisiCaps 允许有意义的联合使用。 内存位置的类型可以在该位置的生命周期内发生变化。

InvisiCaps 在不牺牲功能模型的线程安全性的情况下实现了这一点。

InvisiCaps 最喜欢什么？

InvisiCaps 可以被认为是 SoftBound 的一个实用且完全线程安全的变体。 InvisiCaps 深受这项工作的启发。 与 SoftBound 不同，InvisiCaps 不允许通过指针竞争来逃逸内存安全，并且它们允许使 C 和 C++ 的所有功能都安全，包括诸如函数指针使用之类的微妙内容。 SoftBound 和 InvisiCaps 之间最大的相似之处在于，它们都将功能元数据放置在程序可见的地址空间“外部”。

InvisiCaps 也可以被视为 CHERI 的软件实现。 与 CHERI 不同，InvisiCaps 更具兼容性（指针是 64 位，而不是 128 位或 256 位），并且 InvisiCaps 具有更具确定性的释放后使用故事。

InvisiCaps 的直觉

让我们从简单的直觉开始了解 InvisiCaps 的工作原理，而不用担心指针及其功能如何存储在内存中。 让我们考虑一下：

  • 局部变量中的指针，而不是内存中的指针。 我们称其为飞行指针。
  • 指针所指向的对象的布局。

对于我们的示例，让我们考虑一个指向对象中间的指针。

飞行指针有两部分：

  • 下界 ptr。 这为我们提供了用于边界检查指针访问的下限。 它还指向对象头的正上方，其中包含： 上限 ptr。 我们将使用它进行上限检查。 这必须至少与下界 ptr 一样大。 对于根本无法访问的对象（如自由对象或特殊对象），它可以完全等于 ptr 的下界。 辅助词。 我们会回来讨论这个！ 太狡猾了！
  • ptr 间隔。 这是 C 程序可见的指针的原始整数值。 当 C 程序推理指针的值时（使用算术、强制转换、打印指针、将指针与其他指针进行比较等），它只会看到 intval。

我们将 Flight ptr 的两部分简称为 lower 和 intval（尽管要注意，Fil-C 源代码通常将 intval 称为“ptr”）。

下位不能被C程序修改；当您分配一个对象时，您会得到一个较低的值，然后从分配器出来的指针派生的任何指针都具有该较低值。 下限受到 Fil-C 运行时的信任，因为它用于边界检查（直接用于下限检查，以及间接用于依赖于下限点下方的对象标头的所有检查）。

intval 可以由 C 程序修改，并且根本不被 Fil-C 运行时信任。

每次内存访问都涉及一个不受信任的 intval，指示程序希望访问的位置，以及一个受信任的下级，指示该特定指针可以访问的地址（以及如何访问）。

作为奖励功能，较低的值可以为 NULL。 无法访问具有 NULL 下限的指针（访问时总是会捕获错误，指出您的下限为 NULL）。

如果指针只能存在于局部变量中而不能存储到堆中，那么这就是整个故事！

InvisiCaps 静止状态

当指针存储到堆时，我们将其称为静止指针（或静止指针）。 就像飞行中的指针一样，静止的指针必须知道它的下限和间隔。 用于静止指针的 InvisiCaps 可实现以下目标：

  • 如果您存储指向堆的指针并将其作为整数加载回来，您将获得 intval。
  • 如果将一个整数存储到堆中并将其作为指针加载回来，您将得到一个下限为空的指针，或者存储到该位置的最后一个指针的下限。 您永远不会得到无效或损坏的下层。
  • 无法访问存储下层的位置处的指针；没有能力在其范围内具有用于存储下层（或任何其他 Fil-C 元数据）的位置。 这是 InvisiCaps 的关键属性，也是使它们“不可见”的原因——C 程序只能看到指针的 intval 部分。
  • 指针访问上的竞争会导致程序加载一些有效的较低值（它可能不是您想要的较低值，因此您可能会陷入访问困境）。
  • 原子指针访问（使用 _Atomic、易失性或 std::atomic）实际上是原子的且无锁的。

如何实现这一点背后的关键见解是 InvisiCaps 归纳假设：

每个飞行指针的下限都指向对象标头的顶部，该对象标头的 aux 字包含一种获取存储到该对象有效负载的所有指针的下限的方法。

没有指针的对象的 aux 字就是 NULL。 但是，如果一个对象存储了任何指针，则会分配一个辅助分配，其大小与该对象的有效负载相同。 然后 aux 字指向 aux 分配。 如果我们暂时忽略原子指针，则对象中所有静止的指针都在辅助分配中具有较低值，在对象有效负载中具有 intval。

在此示例中，我们考虑两个对象。 对象#1 在其有效负载中有一个静态指针，该指针指向对象#2。 因此，它的 aux 字指向包含该指针的低位的 aux 分配。 指针的 intval 位于对象的有效负载中。 请注意，我们示例中的飞行指针指向对象 #1 中静止的指针。

因此，所有非原子指针加载和存储都访问 aux 分配（通过 aux 字，位于较低点的下方）和对象有效负载。 存储可能会导致辅助分配被延迟创建。

请注意，这种方法对垃圾收集非常友好：

  • 如果未设置 aux 字，则 GC 会将该对象视为叶子（从 GC 的角度来看，它没有传出指针）。
  • 如果设置了 aux 字，则 GC 仅在 aux 分配中查找指针。

原子隐形帽

但是，如果静态指针是 _Atomic、易失性、std::atomic，或者使用 clang 认为需要原子性的任何其他类型或机制存储，该怎么办？

辅助分配可以包含下级或原子框指针。 辅助分配中的条目类型由该条目的低位确定（0 表示较低，1 表示原子盒指针）。 原子盒是 16 字节、16 字节对齐的分配，其中包含我们使用 128 位原子存储的飞行指针。 或者，如果系统不支持双 CAS，我们可以在原子盒指针本身上使用 64 位原子，并在每次原子突变发生时分配一个新的原子盒（幸运的是 X86_64 和 ARM64 都有 128 位原子，所以这不是必需的）。

请注意，当静态指针是原子指针时，有效负载包含 intval 的副本，纯粹是为了允许整数加载查看 intval。 然而，将整数访问与指针访问竞争可能会导致时间旅行，但不会导致原子盒被破坏——这只是一个逻辑错误。

当程序需要原子性时，这种方法允许 Fil-C 支持原子性 InvisiCaps，而在不要求原子性时使用更便宜的指针方法。

其他注意事项

aux 字仅使用其低 48 位来存储 aux 分配指针；高 16 位用于标志和附加数据。 这些附加数据对于非标准对象分配以及特殊对象（如函数、线程​​和 Fil-C 运行时提供的其他内置类型）很有帮助。 让我们考虑一些例子。

调整分配

如果使用 posix_memalign 或类似的 API 分配对象并且请求足够大的对齐，则较小的值减去对象头大小指向对齐的分配内。 因此，GC 无法仅通过从下限减去对象头大小来计算出分配的基础是什么。 aux 字中的额外标志有足够的空间来编码对象对齐，这可用于计算分配的真正基础。

内存映射

mmap 和 Sys-V 共享内存需要特殊处理。 GC 甚至知道如何管理这些分配，但必须特别小心它们。 如果对象需要这种特殊照顾，则 aux 字具有设置的标志。

函数指针

函数指针有一个 intval 指向实际的函数入口点，而 lower 则指向函数功能：

  • 上限等于下限，这会阻止所有数据访问。
  • 已设置标志来指示该功能确实是一个函数。
  • aux 字的其余部分用于指示真正的函数入口点，以便函数调用可以检查函数指针是否确实指向入口点。

函数指针强制转换是动态处理的（Fil-C 调用约定动态解决调用者传递的类型与被调用者期望的类型之间的不匹配）。

线程数

Fil-C 的内部线程抽象称为 zthread，由 musl 和 glibc 的 pthread 实现在内部使用。 由于zthread包含很多内部运行时状态，因此需要对其进行特殊保护。 指向 zthread 或任何其他内置类型的指针具有以下结构：

  • 下部指向内部 zthread 有效负载。
  • 上限等于下限，因此不可能进行数据访问。
  • aux 字中的标志表明这确实是一个 zthread 对象。

所有采用 zthread 指针的内置函数都会通过查看 aux 字的标志来检查给定的指针是否确实是 zthread 指针。

释放的对象

访问释放的内存时，释放对象会导致确定性恐慌。 这很容易实现：

  • 释放将指针的上限设置为等于下限，从而阻止所有访问。
  • 在 aux 字中设置了一个空闲位，以便在程序发生紧急情况时打印的诊断消息表明这是由于空闲后的使用造成的。

结论

Fil-C使用指针的能力模型来确保内存安全。 为了确保最大程度的兼容性，Fil-C 的 InvisiCap 功能模型给人一种错觉，即指针在 64 位系统上只是 64 位，并允许灵活地重新解释内存类型（包括违反活动成员联合规则），同时保留功能模型本身的健全性。 无论程序多么邪恶，它要么得到内存安全的结果（所有指针访问都在指针合法拥有的能力范围内），要么得到 Fil-C 恐慌。
